content:
# Joshua Chapter 7 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 230-231 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_007_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_007_page_002.jpg`

---

**7:1** And-trespassed [the]-sons of-Israel a-trespass in-the-devoted-thing, and-took Achan son of-Carmi, son of-Zabdi, son of-Zerah, of-[the]-tribe of-Judah, from the-devoted-thing, and-burned [the]-anger of-YHWH against-[the]-sons of-Israel.

**7:2** And-sent Joshua men from Jericho to-the-Ai, which [is] beside Beth-aven, from-east to-Beth-El, and-said to-them, saying, "Go-up and-spy-out the-land." And-went-up the-men, and-they-spied-out the-Ai.

**7:3** And-they-returned to Joshua, and-they-said to-him, "Let not all the-people go-up; about two-thousand men or about three-thousand men let-go-up, and-they-will-strike the-Ai. Do-not-weary there all the-people, for they [are] few."

**7:4** And-went-up from the-people there about three-thousand men, and-they-fled before [the]-men of-the-Ai.

**7:5** And-struck-down from-them [the]-men of-the-Ai about thirty and-six men, and-they-pursued them from-before the-gate unto the-Shebarim, and-they-struck-them-down on-the-descent. And-melted [the]-heart of-the-people, and-it-became as-water.

**7:6** And-tore Joshua his-garments, and-he-fell upon his-face to-the-earth before [the]-ark of-YHWH until the-evening, he and-[the]-elders of-Israel, and-they-put-up dust upon their-heads.

**7:7** And-said Joshua, "Alas, my-Lord YHWH, why did-You-bring bringing this people across the-Jordan, to-give us into-[the]-hand of-the-Amorite, to-destroy us? And-if-only we-had-been-willing and-we-had-dwelt on-[the]-other-side of-the-Jordan!

**7:8** Please, my-Lord, what shall-I-say, after that Israel has-turned [its] back before its-enemies?

**7:9** And-will-hear the-Canaanite and-all [the]-inhabitants of-the-land, and-they-will-surround upon-us, and-they-will-cut-off our-name from the-earth. And-what will-You-do for-Your-name the-great?"

**7:10** And-said YHWH to Joshua, "Rise to-you! Why [is] this -- you [are] falling upon your-face?

**7:11** Sinned Israel, and-also they-have-transgressed My-covenant which I-commanded them, and-also they-have-taken from the-devoted-thing, and-also they-have-stolen, and-also they-have-deceived, and-also they-have-put [it] in-their-vessels.

**7:12** And-not will-be-able [the]-sons of-Israel to-stand before their-enemies; [the]-back they-will-turn before their-enemies, for they-have-become devoted-[to-destruction]. I-will-not continue to-be with-you if you-do-not destroy the-devoted-thing from-your-midst.

**7:13** Rise, sanctify the-people, and-say, 'Sanctify-yourselves for-tomorrow, for thus says YHWH, God of-Israel: A-devoted-thing [is] in-your-midst, Israel; you-are-not-able to-stand before your-enemies until you-remove the-devoted-thing from-your-midst.'

**7:14** And-you-shall-draw-near in-the-morning by-your-tribes, and-it-shall-be, the-tribe which YHWH takes shall-draw-near by-clans, and-the-clan which YHWH takes shall-draw-near by-households, and-the-household which YHWH takes shall-draw-near by-men.

**7:15** And-it-shall-be, the-one-taken with-the-devoted-thing shall-be-burned with-fire, him and-all which [belongs]-to-him, because he-transgressed [the]-covenant of-YHWH, and-because he-did a-disgraceful-thing in-Israel."

**7:16** And-rose-early Joshua in-the-morning, and-he-brought-near Israel by-its-tribes, and-was-taken [the]-tribe of-Judah.

**7:17** And-he-brought-near the-clan of-Judah, and-he-took the-clan of-the-Zerahites. And-he-brought-near the-clan of-the-Zerahites by-men, and-was-taken Zabdi.

**7:18** And-he-brought-near his-household by-men, and-was-taken Achan son of-Carmi, son of-Zabdi, son of-Zerah, of-[the]-tribe of-Judah.

**7:19** And-said Joshua to Achan, "My-son, give, please, glory to-YHWH, God of-Israel, and-give to-Him confession, and-tell, please, to-me, what you-have-done; do-not-hide [it] from-me."

**7:20** And-answered Achan Joshua, and-he-said, "Truly, I -- I have-sinned against-YHWH, God of-Israel, and-like-this and-like-this I-have-done.

**7:21** And-I-saw among-the-spoil one-robe of-Shinar, beautiful, and-two-hundred shekels of-silver, and-one-tongue of-gold -- fifty shekels its-weight -- and-I-coveted them, and-I-took them, and-behold-them hidden in-the-earth in-[the]-midst of-my-tent, and-the-silver beneath it."

**7:22** And-sent Joshua messengers, and-they-ran to the-tent, and-behold, [it was] hidden in-his-tent, and-the-silver beneath it.

**7:23** And-they-took them from-[the]-midst of-the-tent, and-they-brought them to Joshua and-to all [the]-sons of-Israel, and-they-poured-them-out before YHWH.

**7:24** And-took Joshua Achan son of-Zerah, and the-silver, and the-robe, and the-tongue of-gold, and his-sons, and his-daughters, and his-ox, and his-donkey, and his-flock, and his-tent, and all which [belonged]-to-him, and-all Israel with-him, and-they-brought-up them [to the]-Valley of-Achor.

**7:25** And-said Joshua, "Why have-you-troubled us? YHWH will-trouble you this-day." And-stoned him all Israel [with]-stone, and-they-burned them with-fire, and-they-stoned them with-stones.

**7:26** And-they-raised over-him a-heap of-stones, great, until this-day. And-turned-back YHWH from-[the]-burning of-His-anger. Upon-this [the]-name of-that place was-called [the]-Valley of-Achor until this-day.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **7:1 -- Genealogy of Achan:** The MT reads עָכָן בֶּן־כַּרְמִי בֶן־זַבְדִּי בֶן־זֶרַח (Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah). The LXX (Vaticanus) reads Αχαρ (Achar), reflecting 1 Chronicles 2:7 where the name appears as עָכָר. The EMET follows the MT consonantal text.

2. **7:1 -- "Trespassed a trespass" (וַיִּמְעֲלוּ...מַעַל):** The cognate accusative construction (verb + its own noun) is preserved literally per EMET rules.

3. **7:2 -- "The-Ai" (הָעַי):** The Hebrew consistently uses the definite article with Ai (הָעַי), indicating "the-ruin" as a descriptive name. This is preserved throughout.

4. **7:5 -- "The-Shebarim" (הַשְּׁבָרִים):** This may be a proper noun or a common noun meaning "the-broken-places" or "the-quarries." The MT vocalizes it with the article. EMET retains the article as in the source.

5. **7:7 -- Infinitive absolute: "bring bringing" (הַעֲבִיר הֶעֱבַרְתָּ):** The Hiphil infinitive absolute + finite verb is rendered with the doubled construction per EMET rules. Note: the MT has הַעֲבַרְתָּ (Hiphil perfect) with the infinitive הַעֲבִיר, expressing emphasis: "why did You bring bringing."

6. **7:9 -- "Your-name the-great" (שִׁמְךָ הַגָּדוֹל):** Hebrew word order preserved. The adjective follows its noun with the article, standard attributive position.

7. **7:11 -- Fivefold "and-also" (וְגַם):** The MT repeats וְגַם five times in this verse, creating a stacking rhetorical effect: sinned, transgressed, taken, stolen, deceived, put. All conjunctions preserved per EMET rules.

8. **7:12 -- "Devoted-[to-destruction]" (חֵרֶם):** The word חֵרֶם carries the sense of being placed under the ban of total destruction. The bracketed expansion clarifies the Hebrew concept.

9. **7:15 -- "Burned with fire" (יִשָּׂרֵף בָּאֵשׁ):** The MT uses the Niphal of שׂרף. The execution by burning and stoning (v.25) has generated much discussion. The LXX Vaticanus has a somewhat expanded text at this point.

10. **7:17 -- "By-men" (לַגְּבָרִים):** The MT uses לַגְּבָרִים in v.17 for the narrowing from clan to individual. This same process is described in v.14 with the word לַגְּבָרִים.

11. **7:21 -- "One-robe of-Shinar" (אַדֶּרֶת שִׁנְעָר):** Shinar refers to Babylonia. The אַדֶּרֶת is a luxury mantle or cloak. "Tongue of gold" (לְשׁוֹן זָהָב) refers to a bar or wedge shaped like a tongue.

12. **7:23 -- "Poured-them-out" (וַיַּצִּקֻם):** From יצק, to pour out. The stolen goods were spread or laid out before YHWH, likely at the tabernacle.

13. **7:24 -- "Valley of-Achor" (עֵמֶק עָכוֹר):** A wordplay on Achan's name -- עָכוֹר means "trouble" (cf. v.25 עֲכַרְתָּנוּ, "you have troubled us"). The valley name memorializes the event.

14. **7:25 -- Singular then plural:** "And-stoned him...and-they-burned them...and-they-stoned them" -- the shift from singular (Achan) to plural (all his possessions and family) follows the MT exactly. The LXX has a different arrangement of these final clauses.

15. **LXX Vaticanus variants (general):** The LXX text of Joshua 7 contains several expansions and minor variants compared to the MT, including an expanded genealogy in v.1 (adding the father of Zerah) and some rearrangement in the final verses. The EMET follows the MT throughout as its base text.
